page: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-2xL4 is a GPU-accelerated virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 12 vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9575F processor using the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 10.67 GB of RAM per core. For acceleration, the server integrates two NVIDIA L4 GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, delivering a combined 48 GB of VRAM. No local storage is bundled with this instance, and it includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks demonstrate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression both ranking in the top tier. Multi-core CPU performance and database operations fall within the average range. Memory write bandwidth for large blocks is strong at 89.37 GB/sec, while read bandwidth remains average. In language model benchmarks, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 349.81 tokens per second for small models, though prompt processing speeds are average.

From a resource density perspective, the server offers a specialized hardware profile that balances high-performance AMD EPYC processing with dual NVIDIA L4 GPUs. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring dedicated GPU acceleration without the overhead of massive multi-socket CPU configurations. The lack of local storage requires external block storage attachment, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This server is suited for machine learning inference, data science, video processing, and parallel computing tasks.
description: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-2xL4 is a GPU-accelerated virtual server featuring 12 physical cores on an AMD EPYC 9575F x86_64 processor and 128.0 GB of system memory. The server is equipped with two NVIDIA L4 Ada Lovelace GPUs providing a total of 48 GB of VRAM, while local storage is not included. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, alongside strong memory write bandwidth of 89.37 GB/sec. For AI workloads, it delivers top-tier text generation speeds of 349.81 tokens per second on small language models. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for specialized parallel processing, making it highly suitable for machine learning inference, data science pipelines, and video transcoding workloads that require dedicated GPU acceleration without the cost of larger multi-socket CPU configurations.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-2xL4 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 12 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and dual NVIDIA L4 GPUs with 48 GB VRAM, optimized for machine learning inference and data science.
meta_description: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-2xL4 is an x86_64 server with 12 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and two NVIDIA L4 GPUs for accelerated workloads.
tagline: A dual-GPU accelerated virtual server featuring AMD EPYC processors and 128 GB RAM for parallel computing and machine learning inference.
bullet_points: Dual NVIDIA L4 Ada Lovelace GPUs with 48 GB total VRAM
12 AMD EPYC 9575F physical cores with x86_64 architecture
128.0 GB of system memory providing 10.67 GB per core
Top-tier single-core CPU and text generation benchmark performance
Designed for machine learning inference, data science, and parallel processing
